clean_code_rules.name: KISS
clean_code_rules.rule: Avoid unnecessary complexity. Choose the simplest solution that works.

clean_code_rules.name: DRY
clean_code_rules.rule: Avoid code duplication by creating reusable functions or modules.

clean_code_rules.name: YAGNI
clean_code_rules.rule: Do not implement features until they are actually needed.

clean_code_rules.name: Separation of Concerns
clean_code_rules.rule: Separate responsibilities across layers (UI, business logic, data access).

clean_code_rules.name: Single Responsibility Principle
clean_code_rules.rule: A class or function should have only one reason to change.

clean_code_rules.name: Open/Closed Principle
clean_code_rules.rule: Code should be open for extension but closed for modification.

clean_code_rules.name: Liskov Substitution Principle
clean_code_rules.rule: Subclasses must be replaceable for their base classes without breaking behavior.

clean_code_rules.name: Interface Segregation Principle
clean_code_rules.rule: Use small, specific interfaces instead of large general ones.

clean_code_rules.name: Dependency Inversion Principle
clean_code_rules.rule: Depend on abstractions, not on concrete implementations.

clean_code_rules.name: Encapsulation
clean_code_rules.rule: Hide internal implementation details and expose only what is necessary.

clean_code_rules.name: Abstraction
clean_code_rules.rule: Focus on what a component does, not how it does it.

clean_code_rules.name: Law of Demeter
clean_code_rules.rule: Objects should only interact with their immediate dependencies.

clean_code_rules.name: Composition over Inheritance
clean_code_rules.rule: Prefer composition over inheritance for better flexibility.

clean_code_rules.name: Immutability
clean_code_rules.rule: Avoid mutating state directly; prefer immutable data structures.

clean_code_rules.name: Small Functions
clean_code_rules.rule: Functions should be small, focused, and easy to understand.

clean_code_rules.name: Single Level of Abstraction
clean_code_rules.rule: Maintain a consistent level of abstraction within a function.

clean_code_rules.name: Meaningful Naming
clean_code_rules.rule: Use clear, descriptive, and intention-revealing names for variables, functions, and classes.

clean_code_rules.name: Naming Consistency
clean_code_rules.rule: Follow consistent naming conventions (camelCase, PascalCase, etc.) across the codebase.

clean_code_rules.name: Max Line Length
clean_code_rules.rule: Each line of code must not exceed 150 characters. Split long lines into multiple lines for readability.

clean_code_rules.name: File Size Limit
clean_code_rules.rule: Avoid excessively large files. Split files that become too long into smaller modules.

clean_code_rules.name: No Magic Numbers
clean_code_rules.rule: Replace hard-coded numbers with named constants.

clean_code_rules.name: Error Handling
clean_code_rules.rule: Handle errors explicitly and provide meaningful error messages.

clean_code_rules.name: Readability Over Cleverness
clean_code_rules.rule: Prioritize readability over clever or overly concise code.

clean_code_rules.name: Consistency
clean_code_rules.rule: Maintain consistent style, structure, and patterns throughout the project.

clean_code_rules.name: Testing Friendly
clean_code_rules.rule: Write code that is easy to test (low coupling, dependency injection, pure functions).

clean_code_rules.name: No God Object
clean_code_rules.rule: Avoid classes or modules that handle too many responsibilities.

clean_code_rules.name: Limit Parameters
clean_code_rules.rule: Limit the number of function parameters. Use objects if there are too many.

clean_code_rules.name: Avoid Side Effects
clean_code_rules.rule: Minimize hidden side effects within functions.

clean_code_rules.name: Convention Over Configuration
clean_code_rules.rule: Follow established conventions instead of creating custom configurations.

clean_code_rules.name: Low Complexity
clean_code_rules.rule: Keep cyclomatic and cognitive complexity low by simplifying logic and reducing nesting.

clean_code_rules.name: Shallow Nesting
clean_code_rules.rule: Avoid deep nesting. Use early returns or function extraction to simplify control flow.
